prevalence.trend: increasing due to aging, cancer burden, and improved diagnostic access (CTPA)

incidence.source: IHME GBD (VTE) extrapolated + Brazilian hospitalization patterns (DataSUS)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical suspicion with pre-test probability (e.g., Wells), D-dimer (when appropriate), confirmation by CTPA or V/Q scan; DVT ultrasound supportive; follow Brazilian cardiology/pneumology guidance aligned with ESC

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; risk-based prophylaxis in hospitals is variable
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; outcomes depend on timely CTPA access and anticoagulation continuity

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: CTPA availability concentrated in larger hospitals; D-dimer use in APS/UPA varies; referral delays in remote areas
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Anticoagulation (heparin/LMWH then DOACs or warfarin); thrombolysis or thrombectomy for high-risk PE in reference centers; prophylaxis protocols in surgical wards
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Unequal access to CTPA and ICU care, delays in diagnosis, and adherence barriers for long-term anticoagulation follow-up in SUS
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: SUS hospital network can provide acute anticoagulation; growing DOAC access in private/selected public pathways; national guideline alignment with ESC
